lessons.lesson_title: Personal Information and Classroom English: Grammar Lesson 1
lessons.learning_objective: Learners can name, identify, ask and answer basic questions in a grammar task about personal information.
lessons.cefr_outcome: Learners can understand and use familiar everyday expressions and very basic phrases for immediate needs.
lessons.learner_facing_explanation: In this lesson you will practise grammar for personal information and classroom english. You will learn useful language connected to personal information and practise be: am/is/are. By the end, you should be able to complete a short real-life task and explain your answer clearly at A1 level.
lessons.complete_teaching_input: Focus language: be: am/is/are. Vocabulary theme: personal information. The teacher introduces the target language in a meaningful context, models pronunciation and form, checks meaning with concept questions, and moves learners from controlled practice to freer use. At A1, learners should name, identify, ask and answer basic questions with increasing independence. The lesson should use board examples, short teacher modelling, pair practice, feedback and a final communicative task.
examples: Example 1: I can use be: am/is/are to talk about personal information.
Example 2: The learner gives a A1 answer with clear meaning and correct key language.
Example 3: The learner checks, improves and repeats the answer after feedback.
Example 4: The learner uses one linking phrase or support phrase suitable for A1.

interactive_practice.prompt: Choose the best sentence using be: am/is/are.
options: Correct use of be: am/is/are in a A1 sentence.
Incorrect word order with be: am/is/are.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Correct. The sentence uses be: am/is/are accurately.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check the form and meaning of be: am/is/are.

interactive_practice.prompt: Match the personal information phrase to its meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Good matching. You understand the phrase meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Look again at the context and key words.

interactive_practice.prompt: Build a complete answer for the lesson task.
interactive_practice.model_answer: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand personal information.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Well done. Your sentence is complete and meaningful.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check that your sentence has a subject, verb and clear meaning.

correct_answers.P3: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand personal information.

instant_feedback.correct: Correct. Continue to the next step.
instant_feedback.partly_correct: Good start. Check accuracy and add one more detail.
instant_feedback.incorrect: Try again. Review the model and look at the key language.

lessons.teacher_notes: Begin with a quick diagnostic question. Model the target form be: am/is/are. Use choral repetition if pronunciation is important. Monitor pair work and record three common errors. Finish with delayed correction and one confidence-building success point.
lessons.homework_task: Write or record five sentences about personal information using language from this lesson.

assessment_task.task_title: A1 Grammar Check - Personal Information and Classroom English
assessment_task.instructions: Complete a short grammar task using be: am/is/are and vocabulary from personal information.
success_criteria: Uses be: am/is/are with reasonable accuracy
Uses vocabulary connected to personal information
Completes the communicative purpose
Responds at the expected CEFR level

criteria.name: Task achievement
criteria.descriptor: Completes the communicative purpose.

criteria.name: Language control
criteria.descriptor: Uses be: am/is/are and sentence structure accurately.

criteria.name: Vocabulary range
criteria.descriptor: Uses appropriate words connected to personal information.

criteria.name: Fluency/coherence
criteria.descriptor: Organises ideas clearly for the level.

lessons.speaking_task: Speak for 60-120 seconds about personal information. Use at least two examples and one reason.
lessons.writing_task: Write a short A1 response about personal information. Include the target language: be: am/is/are.

listening_audio.script: Audio script for A1-M01-L01. Speaker one: Today we are practising personal information. Speaker two: I need to use be: am/is/are correctly. Speaker one: Listen carefully and choose the answer that best matches the meaning.

visual_activity_cards.title: Be: Am/Is/Are form
visual_activity_cards.alt_text: Visual grammar card for be: am/is/are

lessons.lesson_title: Places, Directions and Local Life: Reading Lesson 3
lessons.learning_objective: Learners can name, identify, ask and answer basic questions in a reading task about places.
lessons.cefr_outcome: Learners can understand and use familiar everyday expressions and very basic phrases for immediate needs.
lessons.learner_facing_explanation: In this lesson you will practise reading for places, directions and local life. You will learn useful language connected to places and practise singular/plural nouns. By the end, you should be able to complete a short real-life task and explain your answer clearly at A1 level.
lessons.complete_teaching_input: Focus language: singular/plural nouns. Vocabulary theme: places. The teacher introduces the target language in a meaningful context, models pronunciation and form, checks meaning with concept questions, and moves learners from controlled practice to freer use. At A1, learners should name, identify, ask and answer basic questions with increasing independence. The lesson should use board examples, short teacher modelling, pair practice, feedback and a final communicative task.
examples: Example 1: I can use singular/plural nouns to talk about places.
Example 2: The learner gives a A1 answer with clear meaning and correct key language.
Example 3: The learner checks, improves and repeats the answer after feedback.
Example 4: The learner uses one linking phrase or support phrase suitable for A1.

interactive_practice.prompt: Choose the best sentence using singular/plural nouns.
options: Correct use of singular/plural nouns in a A1 sentence.
Incorrect word order with singular/plural nouns.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Correct. The sentence uses singular/plural nouns accurately.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check the form and meaning of singular/plural nouns.

interactive_practice.prompt: Match the places phrase to its meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Good matching. You understand the phrase meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Look again at the context and key words.

interactive_practice.prompt: Build a complete answer for the lesson task.
interactive_practice.model_answer: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand places.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Well done. Your sentence is complete and meaningful.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check that your sentence has a subject, verb and clear meaning.

correct_answers.P3: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand places.

instant_feedback.correct: Correct. Continue to the next step.
instant_feedback.partly_correct: Good start. Check accuracy and add one more detail.
instant_feedback.incorrect: Try again. Review the model and look at the key language.

lessons.teacher_notes: Begin with a quick diagnostic question. Model the target form singular/plural nouns. Use choral repetition if pronunciation is important. Monitor pair work and record three common errors. Finish with delayed correction and one confidence-building success point.
lessons.homework_task: Write or record five sentences about places using language from this lesson.

assessment_task.task_title: A1 Reading Check - Places, Directions and Local Life
assessment_task.instructions: Complete a short reading task using singular/plural nouns and vocabulary from places.

Uses vocabulary connected to places

lessons.lesson_title: Travel, Services and Problem Solving: Reading Lesson 3
lessons.learning_objective: Learners can describe, compare and exchange simple information in a reading task about free time.
lessons.cefr_outcome: Learners can communicate in simple and routine tasks requiring direct exchange of information.
lessons.learner_facing_explanation: In this lesson you will practise reading for travel, services and problem solving. You will learn useful language connected to free time and practise going to. By the end, you should be able to complete a short real-life task and explain your answer clearly at A2 level.
lessons.complete_teaching_input: Focus language: going to. Vocabulary theme: free time. The teacher introduces the target language in a meaningful context, models pronunciation and form, checks meaning with concept questions, and moves learners from controlled practice to freer use. At A2, learners should describe, compare and exchange simple information with increasing independence. The lesson should use board examples, short teacher modelling, pair practice, feedback and a final communicative task.
examples: Example 1: I can use going to to talk about free time.
Example 2: The learner gives a A2 answer with clear meaning and correct key language.
Example 3: The learner checks, improves and repeats the answer after feedback.
Example 4: The learner uses one linking phrase or support phrase suitable for A2.

interactive_practice.prompt: Choose the best sentence using going to.
options: Correct use of going to in a A2 sentence.
Incorrect word order with going to.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Correct. The sentence uses going to accurately.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check the form and meaning of going to.

interactive_practice.prompt: Match the free time phrase to its meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Good matching. You understand the phrase meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Look again at the context and key words.

interactive_practice.prompt: Build a complete answer for the lesson task.
interactive_practice.model_answer: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand free time.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Well done. Your sentence is complete and meaningful.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check that your sentence has a subject, verb and clear meaning.

correct_answers.P3: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand free time.

instant_feedback.correct: Correct. Continue to the next step.
instant_feedback.partly_correct: Good start. Check accuracy and add one more detail.
instant_feedback.incorrect: Try again. Review the model and look at the key language.

lessons.teacher_notes: Begin with a quick diagnostic question. Model the target form going to. Use choral repetition if pronunciation is important. Monitor pair work and record three common errors. Finish with delayed correction and one confidence-building success point.
lessons.homework_task: Write or record five sentences about free time using language from this lesson.

assessment_task.task_title: A2 Reading Check - Travel, Services and Problem Solving
assessment_task.instructions: Complete a short reading task using going to and vocabulary from free time.
success_criteria: Uses going to with reasonable accuracy
Uses vocabulary connected to free time
Completes the communicative purpose
Responds at the expected CEFR level

criteria.descriptor: Completes the communicative purpose.

criteria.descriptor: Uses going to and sentence structure accurately.

criteria.name: Fluency/coherence

lessons.learner_facing_explanation: In this lesson you will practise grammar for research, synthesis and critical evaluation. You will learn useful language connected to policy and practise nominalisation. By the end, you should be able to complete a short real-life task and explain your answer clearly at C1 level.
lessons.complete_teaching_input: Focus language: nominalisation. Vocabulary theme: policy. The teacher introduces the target language in a meaningful context, models pronunciation and form, checks meaning with concept questions, and moves learners from controlled practice to freer use. At C1, learners should synthesise, justify, negotiate and adapt register with increasing independence. The lesson should use board examples, short teacher modelling, pair practice, feedback and a final communicative task.
examples: Example 1: I can use nominalisation to talk about policy.
Example 2: The learner gives a C1 answer with clear meaning and correct key language.
Example 3: The learner checks, improves and repeats the answer after feedback.
Example 4: The learner uses one linking phrase or support phrase suitable for C1.

interactive_practice.prompt: Choose the best sentence using nominalisation.
options: Correct use of nominalisation in a C1 sentence.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Correct. The sentence uses nominalisation accurately.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check the form and meaning of nominalisation.

interactive_practice.prompt: Match the policy phrase to its meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Good matching. You understand the phrase meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Look again at the context and key words.

interactive_practice.prompt: Build a complete answer for the lesson task.
interactive_practice.model_answer: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand policy.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Well done. Your sentence is complete and meaningful.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check that your sentence has a subject, verb and clear meaning.

correct_answers.P3: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand policy.

instant_feedback.correct: Correct. Continue to the next step.
instant_feedback.partly_correct: Good start. Check accuracy and add one more detail.
instant_feedback.incorrect: Try again. Review the model and look at the key language.

lessons.teacher_notes: Begin with a quick diagnostic question. Model the target form nominalisation. Use choral repetition if pronunciation is important. Monitor pair work and record three common errors. Finish with delayed correction and one confidence-building success point.

lessons.learner_facing_explanation: In this lesson you will practise writing for c1 review, portfolio and performance assessment. You will learn useful language connected to analysis and practise hedging. By the end, you should be able to complete a short real-life task and explain your answer clearly at C1 level.
lessons.complete_teaching_input: Focus language: hedging. Vocabulary theme: analysis. The teacher introduces the target language in a meaningful context, models pronunciation and form, checks meaning with concept questions, and moves learners from controlled practice to freer use. At C1, learners should synthesise, justify, negotiate and adapt register with increasing independence. The lesson should use board examples, short teacher modelling, pair practice, feedback and a final communicative task.
examples: Example 1: I can use hedging to talk about analysis.
Example 2: The learner gives a C1 answer with clear meaning and correct key language.

lessons.lesson_title: Specialised Academic and Professional English: Reading Lesson 3
lessons.learning_objective: Learners can critique, refine, interpret nuance and produce expert-level responses in a reading task about abstract argument.
lessons.cefr_outcome: Learners can understand virtually everything heard or read and express themselves precisely in complex situations.
lessons.learner_facing_explanation: In this lesson you will practise reading for specialised academic and professional english. You will learn useful language connected to abstract argument and practise register control. By the end, you should be able to complete a short real-life task and explain your answer clearly at C2 level.
lessons.complete_teaching_input: Focus language: register control. Vocabulary theme: abstract argument. The teacher introduces the target language in a meaningful context, models pronunciation and form, checks meaning with concept questions, and moves learners from controlled practice to freer use. At C2, learners should critique, refine, interpret nuance and produce expert-level responses with increasing independence. The lesson should use board examples, short teacher modelling, pair practice, feedback and a final communicative task.
examples: Example 1: I can use register control to talk about abstract argument.
Example 2: The learner gives a C2 answer with clear meaning and correct key language.
Example 3: The learner checks, improves and repeats the answer after feedback.
Example 4: The learner uses one linking phrase or support phrase suitable for C2.

interactive_practice.prompt: Choose the best sentence using register control.
options: Correct use of register control in a C2 sentence.
Incorrect word order with register control.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Correct. The sentence uses register control accurately.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check the form and meaning of register control.

interactive_practice.prompt: Match the abstract argument phrase to its meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Good matching. You understand the phrase meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Look again at the context and key words.

interactive_practice.prompt: Build a complete answer for the lesson task.
interactive_practice.model_answer: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand abstract argument.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Well done. Your sentence is complete and meaningful.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check that your sentence has a subject, verb and clear meaning.

correct_answers.P3: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand abstract argument.

instant_feedback.correct: Correct. Continue to the next step.
instant_feedback.partly_correct: Good start. Check accuracy and add one more detail.
instant_feedback.incorrect: Try again. Review the model and look at the key language.

lessons.teacher_notes: Begin with a quick diagnostic question. Model the target form register control. Use choral repetition if pronunciation is important. Monitor pair work and record three common errors. Finish with delayed correction and one confidence-building success point.
lessons.homework_task: Write or record five sentences about abstract argument using language from this lesson.
